rulings.Case Title: RONALD ANTHONY YATES VS. KATHRYN R. DEVINCENZI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 12, 2021 line 1. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED in its entirety. The only claim that plaintiffs seek to adjudicate is their first cause of action for partition. There is a triable disputes on that cause of action whether a one-third interest in the property sought to be partitioned is owned by Ronald Yates as a sole trustee or by Ronald Yates and Barbara Yates as joint as co-trustees and, if owned by the co-trustees, whether Ms. Yates is unavailable to perform her duties as co-trustee. Plaintiffs' separate statement, which is the due process document notifying Ms. Devincenzi what facts she must controvert to defeat the motion states that the one-third interest is owned by the co-trustees and makes no mention of Ms. Yates being unavailable to perform her duties as co-trustee. The evidence submitted with the reply papers was not considered. (Jay v. Mahaffey (2013) 218 Cal. App. 4th 1522, 1536-38). =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: PETER DINH VS. ROB MILLS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Special Motion And Special Motion To Strike
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 12, 2021 line 2. DEFENDANT ROB MILLS' NOTICE OF SPECIAL MOTION AND SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE is GRANTED. Mr. Mills satisfied his first prong burden showing that both of the causes of action alleged by plaintiff Peter Dinh arise out of protected activity. Both causes of action arise from the allegations that Mr. Mills asked Mr. Dinh to sign a new lease without knowledge whether the new lease was materially the same as the old lease and Mr. Mills sought to terminate Mr. Dinh's tenancy based on Mr. Dinh's refusal to sign the new lease. Both of these actions are protected activity by the anti-SLAPP statute. Mr. Dinh failed to satisfy his second prong burden to show that there is minimal merit to either of his causes of action. The admissible evidence shows that the actions taken by Mr. Mills were not done in bad faith and are immunized from liability by the litigation privilege and Mr. Dinh has presented no admissible evidence otherwise. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
